summary: Cathy O'Neil identifies the new FEMA model used during the COVID-19 pandemic as a 'Weapon of Math Destruction' (WMD). She argues that this algorithm, which was reportedly used to pressure Arizona's governor into reopening early, meets her three criteria for a WMD: it's important (affects life-and-death decisions), secret (not open to public scrutiny), and destructive (potentially causing harm). The post links to her Bloomberg column calling for public transparency in reopening decisions.
key_points: The FEMA model used to guide state reopening decisions qualifies as a 'Weapon of Math Destruction'
The algorithm was allegedly used to pressure Arizona's governor into reopening early
WMDs are defined by three characteristics: important, secret, and destructive
Decisions affecting public health should involve public data and open debate rather than opaque algorithms
money_quotes: The new FEMA model – which was used to strong-arm the Arizona governor into opening early – is a WMD, i.e. important, secret, and destructive
A Secret Algorithm Is Deciding Who Will Die in America
Decisions on reopening should involve public data and debate
key_insight: Government algorithms that make life-and-death decisions without public transparency are dangerous weapons that undermine democratic accountability.
